subject: Gaming
title: Daily Login Rewards and Habit Loops
description: A quick, practical guide to Daily Login Rewards and Habit Loops.

hook.text: Miss one day and your streak resets. Daily login systems turn games into daily obligations.

buildup.text: Charles Duhigg's habit loop—cue, routine, reward—is exactly what daily login systems create.

discovery.text: Streaks exploit loss aversion. Breaking a 30-day streak feels like losing, not just stopping.

twist.text: Players report logging into games they don't enjoy just to maintain streaks. Fun is no longer the point.

climax.text: Daily systems prioritize engagement metrics over player enjoyment—a fundamental misalignment.

punchline.text: Log in or lose your streak. The game became a daily chore.

quiz.question: Why do daily login streaks feel so hard to break?
options: Loss aversion makes breaking a streak feel like losing progress
Players genuinely enjoy logging in every day
